stories.headline: S&P 500 AND NASDAQ HIT NEW ALL-TIME HIGHS; NASDAQ ON 11-DAY WIN STREAK
stories.body: S&P 500 +0.80% to 7,022; Nasdaq +1.59% to 24,016. The Nasdaq's strongest 11-day run on record reflects broad strength in big tech and AI-related sectors, even as geopolitical tensions keep volatility in play. The Dow edged lower by 0.15%, but breadth remains strong.

stories.headline: TSMC CRUSHES Q1 EARNINGS WITH 58% PROFIT SURGE; AI DEMAND 'EXTREMELY ROBUST'
stories.body: Taiwan Semiconductor's first-quarter profit jumped 58% YoY, crushing analyst expectations as AI chip demand remains insatiable. CEO C.C. Wei flagged potential supply chain impacts from Iran conflict (helium shortages), but current trajectory supports forward guidance. Validates strong corporate earnings power.

stories.headline: PEPSICO BEATS EXPECTATIONS; PRICING POWER INTACT AMID COST PRESSURES
stories.body: PepsiCo reported Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.61 vs. $1.55 consensus, and revenue of $19.44B vs. $18.95B expected. Strong beat on both lines suggests the company's pricing actions are sticking despite inflationary headwinds. Upcoming: Ally Financial and Regions Financial report Friday morning.

stories.headline: OIL RALLIES ON IRAN-HORMUZ TENSIONS; WTI +1.1% AS GEOPOLITICAL RISK PREMIUM PERSISTS
stories.body: WTI crude +1.1% to $92/barrel as Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed with US naval presence. Brent around $94.89 (-0.04%). Supply disruption fears keep energy markets supported, though downstream impacts on margins for airlines and industrials remain a debate among investors.

stories.headline: GOLD RALLIES TO $4,830 ON WEAK DOLLAR AND SAFE-HAVEN BID; DXY AT SIX-WEEK LOW
stories.body: Gold +0.8% to $4,830/oz as the DXY (US Dollar Index) slides 0.3% to 98.70, a six-week low. Inverse relationship between currency strength and precious metals is textbook—when the dollar weakens, foreign investors buy gold more readily, and gold becomes cheaper in other currencies.

stories.headline: WARRIORS SURVIVE CLIPPERS IN PLAY-IN THRILLER; CURRY DROPS 35 IN RETURN FROM INJURY
stories.body: Golden State 126, LA Clippers 121. Stephen Curry, just 10 days back from knee injury, erupted for 35 points (27 in second half) as the Warriors advance in the play-in tournament. Curry hit the go-ahead 3-pointer with under two minutes. Warriors visit Phoenix Suns on Friday.

stories.headline: 76ERS SECURE 7TH SEED WITH PLAY-IN WIN OVER MAGIC; MAXEY ERUPTS FOR 31
stories.body: Philadelphia 75, Orlando 69. Tyrese Maxey's 31 points powered the 76ers to clinch the 7th seed in the East and a first-round date with No. 2 Boston Celtics. Magic's season ends despite strong regular-season push. Eastern Conference bracket is now set.

stories.headline: NFL DRAFT ARRIVING APRIL 23-25 IN PITTSBURGH; SEAHAWKS TO PICK 32ND OVERALL
stories.body: With the draft one week away, the Seahawks hold the 32nd pick and four total selections. Top needs: RB (after Kenneth Walker III free agency loss), edge rusher, and cornerback depth. Mel Kiper projects Notre Dame RB Jadarian Price at 32. Draft coverage begins April 23.

stories.headline: SPRING GAMES IN FULL SWING; TEXAS A&M MAROON & WHITE GAME TODAY
stories.body: College football spring season reaches crescendo today with Texas A&M's Maroon & White spring finale in College Station. Over 40 Power Four programs are holding spring games this week as coaches evaluate depth and QB battles ahead of the 2026 regular season.

sports.detail: In spring practice window. Spring preview on RBs published April 14. Regular season begins Sept 2026.
Zoom Diallo entered transfer portal April 3. Off-season mode. Season begins Nov 2026.
32nd overall pick in draft April 23-25 (Pittsburgh). Needs: RB, edge, CB. Recently won Super Bowl.
Last game Apr 4: beat 76ers 116-93. Likely in playoffs. Check standings for current status.
Game today 8:40 PM PT vs San Diego at Petco Park. Record: 4-9 (1-5 away). Recent: beat Astros 8-7.

watchlist: Ally Financial Q1 earnings Friday 8:30 AM ET — watch for regional banking margin pressure from higher energy
Regions Financial earnings Friday — another regional bank barometer for net interest margin health
Mariners at Padres tonight 8:40 PM PT — T-Mobile Park next home game Saturday vs Angels

deepDive.title: Why Record Equities Ignore Sticky Yields: The Earnings Power Thesis
content: Today the S&P 500 hit another all-time high at 7,022 even though the 10-year Treasury sits at 4.31%—a level that would normally compress equity valuations to oblivion. The traditional relationship is simple: higher discount rates mean lower present values for future cash flows, so stocks should fall when bonds yield more. But here's the puzzle nobody is discussing clearly: equities hit records *because* earnings are growing faster than the market expected. TSMC's 58% profit surge and PepsiCo's earnings beat prove that corporate pricing power is intact despite geopolitical chaos. When earnings outpace discount-rate increases, valuations can expand even at higher yields.

The mechanism is pure finance: stock value equals discounted future earnings. Two variables move simultaneously—the numerator (earnings growth) and denominator (discount rate). When the 10Y yields 4.31%, we discount future cash flows at higher rates, which mechanically lowers valuation multiples. But earnings that beat by 10% globally can more than offset that denominator effect. The equity risk premium—the excess return you demand to own stocks over risk-free Treasuries—is roughly 70 basis points right now (if equities earn ≈5% real and bonds yield 4.31%). That margin is still historically reasonable for taking equity risk, which explains why the Nasdaq just posted an 11-day win streak. Investors are saying: growth is real, pricing power persists, the earnings yield justifies the risk.

Geopolitical shocks normally break this thesis. The Iran-Hormuz situation has WTI at $92, which *should* compress corporate margins through input cost inflation. But here's what the market is pricing: margin pain is *sector-specific*, not universal. Airlines and trucking lose; refineries and renewable energy makers win. Tech companies like TSMC have enough pricing power to pass costs downstream, and AI demand is so hot that they're booking record orders anyway. The Nasdaq's strength today reflects confidence that the earnings growth trajectory for high-margin software, semiconductors, and cloud companies will more than offset the headwind from energy-intensive sectors. That's not complacency—it's a differentiated bet on sector-level resilience.

Watch Friday's Ally Financial and Regions Financial earnings closely. Regional banks are the canary in the coal mine for margin compression: if their net interest margins are under pressure from higher deposit costs, or if loan losses spike from energy-sector weakness, that signals the earnings thesis is starting to fray. Today's record highs assume earnings *growth* persists despite geopolitical risk. If Friday's bank earnings show that cost pressures are eating into profitability, the market will begin repricing the equity risk premium downward, and valuations will compress. Until then, sticky yields + rising equities = a market betting on earnings power, not Fed cuts.
